steps.title: Locate the purring trait
steps.content: The text says rusty-spotted cats have a rigid hyoid bone that rumbles when the larynx vibrates, producing a purr. Lions, by contrast, have a more flexible hyoid and a stretchy ligament that help produce roars.

steps.title: Reject unsupported traits
steps.content:
• Choice A names a ligament that rusty-spotted cats lack.
  • Choice B mentions body size, but the text identifies the hyoid as the proposed cause of purring.
  • Choice D mentions larynx shape, which the text does not identify as the relevant trait.
The remaining choice names the rigid hyoid bone. The answer is C.
